# dotfiles

> Personal dotfiles managed by chezmoi. Bash-based CLI utilities use options.bash for option parsing and rich terminal output. Targets Ubuntu (Debian), Red Hat-like distros (Fedora, RHEL, CentOS, Rocky, Alma), and macOS.

## Chezmoi config

Template data from `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`:
- `osId` тАФ exact OS (e.g., `linux-ubuntu`, `linux-fedora`, `darwin`)
- `osIdLike` тАФ OS family (e.g., `linux-debian`, `linux-rhel`, `darwin`)
- `osFamily` тАФ `linux` or `darwin`
- `pkgManager` тАФ `apt`, `dnf`, or `brew-only` (derived from `osIdLike`)
- `wsl` тАФ bool, running under WSL
- `codespaces` тАФ bool, running in GitHub Codespaces
- `hasGui` тАФ bool, machine has GUI

## Bootstrap: bootstrap.sh

`~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh` auto-updates options.bash and sources core modules:

```bash
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/ansi.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-version.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-help.sh
```

Defines `echoRun` / `echoRun --bold` (colored command echo + eval).
Aliases `doas` as `sudo` when available.

## CLI utilities (options.bash-based)

All source `~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh`. Pattern:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euCo pipefail
shopt -s expand_aliases
. ~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh
args::program "$script_name" "1.0" "Description"
args::option "-v, --version" "Show version"
args::option "-h, --help" "Show help"
args::parse "$@"
set -- "${args_cleaned[@]}"
```

### arx

Archive viewer/extractor.

Commands:
- `arx archive.tar.gz` тАФ list contents (default)
- `arx list archive.tar.gz` / `arx l archive.tar.gz` тАФ list contents
- `arx extract archive.tar.gz` тАФ extract to current directory

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Supported formats: `.tar.br`, `.tar.bz2`, `.tar.gz`, `.tar.xz`, `.tar.zst`, `.tar`, `.tbz2`, `.tgz`, `.7z`, `.deb`, `.rar`, `.zip`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`, `.Z`

### Playbash Server Management

Multi-host bash playbook runner (v1тАУv3 complete). Replaced the previous Ansible-based stack. See `dev-docs/playbash-design.md`.

**Runner:** Node CLI at `~/.local/bin/playbash` (single entrypoint + modules under `~/.local/share/playbash/`: `runner.js`, `render.js`, `inventory.js`, `sidecar.js`, `staging.js`, `transfer.js`, `commands.js`, `doctor.js`, `errors.js`, `paths.js`, `shell-escape.js`, `subprocess.js`, `ssh-config.js`, `completion.bash`). Cross-platform PTY allocation via `~/.local/libs/playbash-wrap.py` (Python, with stdin relay for `--sudo`). Linux + macOS targets supported end-to-end.

**Subcommands** (targets always come first):
- `playbash run <targets> <playbook>` тАФ run with live rectangle (single target) or status board (multiple targets)
- `playbash push <targets> <script-path>` тАФ upload a local script and run it (always uploads)
- `playbash debug <targets> <playbook>` тАФ same as `run` with `-n 0` and verbose summary
- `playbash exec <targets> [--] <command...>` тАФ one-shot command, no playbook
- `playbash put <targets> <local-path> [<remote-path>]` тАФ upload a file or directory
- `playbash get <targets> <remote-path> [<local-path>]` тАФ download a file or directory
- `playbash list` / `playbash hosts` / `playbash log [host] [command]`

**Options:** `-n/--lines N` (rectangle height, default 5; 0 for exec), `-p/--parallel N`, `--self`, `--sudo` (prompt once for a password; for run/exec/push/debug injects via PTY stdin relay; for put/get wraps remote commands with `sudo -S` and prepends the password to stdin), `--report` (write plain-text summary of actionable events to stdout; for run/push only), `-N/--no-precheck` (skip offline detection).

**Targets:** comma-separated host names, group names, the implicit `all` group, and the special `@self` pseudo-host (resolves to local hostname, implies `--self`). Resolved from `~/.config/playbash/inventory.json`. Inventory hosts are **managed** (playbooks pre-deployed via chezmoi); bare ssh aliases get the playbook pushed automatically. `put`/`get`/`push` support `{host}` template expansion in paths. Custom script paths (containing `/`) in `run`/`push` always use upload mode.

**Offline detection:** pre-flight `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=2 true` parallelized across all targets. Offline hosts get a distinct status, excluded from failure counts. `-N` to skip.

**Reporting model:** playbooks emit JSON-lines events into a per-run sidecar (`$PLAYBASH_REPORT`). Two helper libraries: `~/.local/libs/playbash.sh` (`playbash_info`/`warn`/`error`/`action`/`reboot`/`step`) and `~/.local/libs/maintenance.sh` (`report_reboot`/`report_warn`/`report_action`). Both pretty-print to stderr when `$PLAYBASH_REPORT` is unset.

**Logs:** at `~/.cache/playbash/runs/<host>/<command>/<timestamp>.log`. `playbash log [host] [command]` finds the most recent.

**Periodic tasks:** each managed host runs its own timer via `setup-periodic`. The primary scheduled command is `playbash run @self daily --report` (local run, no SSH). Notification wrappers handle failure emails. See `dev-docs/periodic-tasks-design.md`.

### setup-periodic

Set up periodic scheduled tasks. Creates systemd timers (Linux) or launchd system daemons (macOS).

Commands:
- `setup-periodic add daily "4:00 mo-sa" "playbash run @self daily --report"` тАФ create daily timer
- `setup-periodic add weekly "4:00 su" "playbash run @self weekly --report"` тАФ create weekly timer
- `setup-periodic list` тАФ show active periodic tasks
- `setup-periodic remove daily` тАФ remove a task

Schedule format: `HH:MM`, `Nh`, or `Nm`, optionally followed by day spec (`mo-fr`, `tu,th`, `1,15`).

Options:
- `--email ADDRESS` тАФ notification email (default: chezmoi-configured email; with `add`)
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Prerequisites: requires `sendmail` (msmtp). Fails with install instructions if missing.

Fleet-wide setup: `playbash exec all -- setup-periodic add daily "4:00 mo-sa" "playbash run @self daily --report"`

### Notification wrappers

**notify-playbash** тАФ runs `playbash <args> --report`, emails if exit non-zero OR report non-empty. Silent on clean success. Used by `setup-periodic` for playbash tasks.

**notify-on-failure** тАФ generic wrapper for launchd/cron. Runs a command, captures output, emails on non-zero exit. Silent on success.

**notify-systemd-failure** тАФ systemd `OnFailure=` handler. Called by `email-notify@.service` template unit. Reads `journalctl --user-unit` for the failed unit and emails via `sendmail`.

**email-notify@.service** тАФ systemd template unit at `~/.config/systemd/user/`. Wired via `OnFailure=email-notify@%n.service` on generic task services. Linux only.

### upd

System updater. Template: `executable_upd.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-c, --clean` тАФ run `cln` after updating
- `-y, --yes` тАФ assume yes to all prompts
- `-r, --restart-services` тАФ after a `docker-ce`/`containerd`/`docker-buildx-plugin`/`docker-compose-plugin` upgrade, run `sudo systemctl restart containerd && sudo systemctl restart docker` to recover without a full reboot (falls back to a reboot recommendation if either restart fails). NOPASSWD whitelist entries for these commands ship in `run_onchange_after_install-sudoers.sh.tmpl` (and `run_onchange_after_install-doas.sh.tmpl` if doas is installed).
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Updates: `apt` (Debian), `dnf` (Red Hat-like), `snap` (Debian), `flatpak`, `softwareupdate` (macOS), `mas` (macOS), `brew`, `tmux` plugins, `bun`.

Sources `~/.local/libs/maintenance.sh` for `report_*` helpers and apt-history scanning. After the apt step, `maintenance::check_apt_since` diffs `/var/log/apt/history.log` since the pre-step snapshot and reacts: docker-related upgrades тЖТ restart-or-reboot, AppArmor upgrades тЖТ eager `aa-remove-unknown` + recovery marker file at `~/.cache/playbash/needs-aa-cleanup`, `/run/reboot-required` тЖТ `report_reboot` with the package list as the reason. All events flow into `$PLAYBASH_REPORT` when running under `playbash`.

### cln

System cleanup. Template: `executable_cln.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-y, --yes` тАФ assume yes to all prompts
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Cleans: `apt autoremove/autoclean` (Debian), `dnf autoremove` + `dnf clean all` (Red Hat-like), `flatpak uninstall --unused`, `brew autoremove/cleanup`, `docker system prune`, node version management.

### jot

Encrypted S3 notes editor.

Commands:
- `jot` тАФ list files (default)
- `jot file.md.gz.age` / `jot edit file` / `jot e file` тАФ edit file
- `jot list [prefix]` / `jot l [prefix]` тАФ list files
- `jot get file` / `jot g file` тАФ download to current directory
- `jot put local_file remote_name` / `jot p ...` тАФ upload file
- `jot delete file` / `jot del file` / `jot rm file` / `jot d file` тАФ delete
- `jot rename old new` / `jot mv old new` / `jot r old new` тАФ rename

Options:
- `-x, --exact` тАФ exact file name match (not prefix)
- `-y, --yes` тАФ create new file without confirmation
- `-n, --no` тАФ don't create new file
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Environment variables:
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET` тАФ AWS bucket name (required)
- `JOT_AGE_KEY` тАФ age key path (default: `~/.ssh/age.key`)
- `JOT_SSH_PUB` тАФ SSH public key (default: `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`)
- `JOT_SSH_PRV` тАФ SSH private key (default: `~/.ssh/id_rsa`)

Supported codecs: `.age`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`

### goup

Run a command in the current directory and each parent directory up to `/`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `goup "make test"` тАФ runs `make test` in `.`, `..`, `../..`, etc.

### ollama-sync

Update all installed ollama models.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

### mount-raid

Mount a remote NFS filesystem. Template: `executable_mount-raid.tmpl`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `mount-raid server-name`

## CLI utilities (standalone)

### gpurr

Git pull with submodule recursion + pull main branch in each submodule.

### gpwiki

Git pull + pull main in `wiki/` submodule if present.

### update-dependencies

Clone or update `options.bash` sparse worktree at `~/.local/share/libs/scripts`.

### git-br

Pretty-formatted `git branch` with date, author, subject.

### git-brr

Remote branches sorted by committer date (`git-br --remote --sort=-committerdate`).

### git-bs

Branch list sorted by author date in columnar format.

### git-mbs

Filter `git-bs --all` to show only branches by the current git user.

### git-pull-main

Checkout and pull the default branch (auto-detected from `origin`).

### git-super-clean

`git reset --hard` + `git clean --force -d -x`.

## Shell aliases (dot_bash_aliases)

Key aliases and functions:

### File listing (eza)
- `l` тЖТ `eza`
- `ls` тЖТ `eza --grid --color auto --icons --sort=type`
- `ll` тЖТ `eza --long ...`
- `la` тЖТ `eza --grid --all ...`
- `lla` тЖТ `eza --long --all ...`
- `ltr` тЖТ `eza -l -t modified -r`
- `l.` тЖТ list dotfiles only

### Navigation (zoxide)
- `..` тЖТ `z ..`
- `...` тЖТ `z ../../`
- `up N` тЖТ go up N directories
- `zl [dir]` тЖТ `z dir && l`
- `mkz dir` тЖТ `mkdir dir && z dir`

### File operations
- `rm` тЖТ `rm -I --preserve-root`
- `mv` тЖТ `mv -iv`
- `cp` тЖТ `cp -iv`
- `rcp` / `rmv` тЖТ rsync copy/move with aggregate progress
- `rup` / `rsy` тЖТ rsync update / sync

### Git
- `gst` тЖТ `git status`
- `gco` тЖТ `git checkout`
- `gcob` тЖТ `git checkout -b`
- `gcm` тЖТ `git commit`
- `gbr` тЖТ `git branch`
- `gpull` тЖТ `git pull`
- `gpush` тЖТ `git push`
- `gsw` тЖТ `git switch` (default branch if no args)
- `gme` тЖТ `git merge` (default branch if no args)

### Better replacements
- `cat` тЖТ `bat` / `batcat`
- `ping` тЖТ `sudo prettyping`
- `top` тЖТ `sudo htop`
- `du` тЖТ `ncdu`
- `help` тЖТ `tldr`

### SSH helpers
- `ssht host [session]` тЖТ SSH + tmux attach/new
- `mosht host [session]` тЖТ mosh + tmux
- `ett host [session]` тЖТ Eternal Terminal + tmux
- `kssh` тЖТ `kitten ssh`
- `kssht host [session]` тЖТ kitten ssh + tmux

### Other
- `where "pattern" [path/wildcard]` тАФ recursive grep in files
- `upfind` / `upfd` тАФ search up directory tree
- `upsearch` тЖТ `upfd -gH` (no glob expansion)
- `tre` тЖТ `tree` excluding common junk dirs
- `gre` тЖТ `grep -r` excluding common junk dirs
- `oports` тЖТ list open ports
- `psr` / `psm` / `psg` / `pst` тАФ process listing variants
- `nowrap [col] [-col]` тАФ truncate output to terminal width

## Environment

Set in `dot_bashrc`:
- `EDITOR=micro`
- `VISUAL=micro`
- `GITHUB_USERNAME=uhop`
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET=${GITHUB_USERNAME}-notes`
- `FORCE_COLOR=3`

## Links

- Chezmoi: https://www.chezmoi.io/
- options.bash: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash
- options.bash wiki: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash/wiki
